name: Skillz (Firy Inc.)
model: Developer platform — hosts matchmaking/tournament infrastructure for third-party mobile games; entry-fee rake on head-to-head/tournament play
flagship: Solitaire Cube, Blackout Bingo, Bubble Shooter
status: Public (NYSE: FIRY); rebranded from Skillz Inc. to Firy Inc. holding company (Jun 2026) spanning Skillz gaming, RZR ad-tech, and Beamable infrastructure units; won $42.9M (v. AviaGames, 2024) and $420M (v. Papaya, 2026) jury verdicts; core gaming segment still cash-burning

name: Papaya Gaming
model: First-party developer — reskins classic casual games as small-stakes real-money tournaments
flagship: Solitaire Cash, Bubble Cash, Bingo Cash, 21 Cash
status: Private; found liable for $420M in Skillz's false-advertising suit (Apr 2026); $15M consumer class-action settlement (Oct 2025); Michigan cease-and-desist (Oct 2024)

name: AviaGames
model: First-party developer — Pocket7Games tournament platform (bingo, solitaire, arcade)
status: Private; lost $42.9M jury verdict to Skillz (Feb 2024, patent infringement + bot allegations); subject of an active DOJ criminal grand jury probe re: bot use

name: Triumph Labs
model: Developer SDK — free to integrate; Triumph takes a 20% cut of tournament entry fees

name: WorldWinner / Game Taco
model: Legacy first-party skill-game tournament portal; licensed arcade/casino-category games
status: Private; Game Taco acquired WorldWinner from GSN Games (Sony Pictures), a Platinum Equity-backed deal (May 2021); renamed itself WorldWinner (Oct 2023)

name: Voodoo / Blitz - Win Cash
model: Hyper-casual publisher; Blitz is its real-money tournament arm
flagship: Blitz - Win Cash (Bingo, Solitaire, pool, match-3)
status: Private; sued by Skillz (Jul 2024) over alleged bot use despite a 'no bots allowed' marketing policy

name: MPL (Mobile Premier League)
model: Multi-game platform (fantasy sports, puzzle, casual, board games)
status: Private unicorn; FY23 revenue $104.6M, $37M net loss; real-money operations suspended after India's Aug 2025 RMG ban

name: WinZO
model: Horizontal marketplace — hosts third-party multiplayer games across casual/card/esports/Battle Royale categories, 12 languages
flagship: WinZO app — third-party multiplayer titles across casual, card, esports and Battle Royale categories, in 12 languages
status: Private; co-founders arrested by India's Enforcement Directorate (Nov 2025) on money-laundering charges tied partly to continuing real-money play post-ban

name: Zupee
model: First-party board/trivia games (Ludo-style) as real-money tournaments

name: Dream11 (Dream Sports)
model: Fantasy sports (primarily cricket); entry-fee take-rate on fantasy contests
totalRaisedNote: Derived arithmetic: >$720M total-to-date raise after the Mar 2021 round (per wsc-dreamsports-round-2021-03's quote, 'brings Dream Sports' total to-date raise to over $720 million') + $840M Nov 2021 round (wsc-dreamsports-round-2021-11) = >=$1.56B; the 'over $720M' phrasing makes this a floor, not an exact total
status: Private; paused all paid contests (>90% of revenue) after India's Aug 2025 RMG ban

name: DraftKings
model: Sportsbook + DFS (peer-to-peer fantasy); entry-fee rake (DFS), vig/hold (sportsbook)
status: Public (Nasdaq: DKNG); FTC blocked its 2016-announced merger with FanDuel (abandoned Jul 2017)

name: FanDuel
hq: Edinburgh, Scotland (founded); operated under Flutter Entertainment
status: Majority-owned by Flutter Entertainment (~77.4%), Fox Corp (17.6%), Boyd Gaming (5%); $5.8B 2024 revenue

name: PrizePicks
model: Peer-to-peer 'Arena' pick'em (pivoted fully to P2P Aug 2025, abandoning against-the-house pick'em)
status: Private, pending Allwyn majority acquisition (62.3% stake, $1.6B cash / up to $4.15B EV, announced Sept 2025, expected H1 2026 close); $339M trailing-12mo adjusted EBITDA

name: Sleeper
model: Social fantasy sports + real-money 'Sleeper Picks' pick'em product

name: VGW (Chumba Casino, LuckyLand Slots, Global Poker)
model: Sweepstakes casino — house-banked RNG games (Gold-Coin/Sweeps-Coin sales), not peer-to-peer
flagship: Chumba Casino, LuckyLand Slots, Global Poker
status: Private, founder-owned; founder Laurence Escalante resigned amid criminal charges (Jul 2026); sued by Louisiana over unpaid taxes (Sept 2025)
note: house-banked casino-style — a different legal workaround (sweepstakes coins), not P2P

name: Stake.us
model: Sweepstakes casino — house-banked RNG games (Gold Coins/Stake Cash), not peer-to-peer
status: Private (EasyGo group); named in first-ever US government sweepstakes-casino civil enforcement suit (LA City Attorney, Aug 2025)
note: house-banked casino-style — a different legal workaround (sweepstakes coins), not P2P

name: Toast (Carnival)
model: B2B/B2B2C skill-game studio and platform — builds proprietary multiplayer skill games and licenses/white-labels a platform to operators, plus its own house-brand consumer app (Carnival)
flagship: 21 Hustle (PvP Blackjack), Gin Rummy, Pinfall, Basketball Hustle, Cricket PowerPlay
status: Private, pre-seed; per company/call claim, the only PEER-TO-PEER entrant in the casino-stakes x real-time quadrant (~$100 hands) — VGW/Stake.us occupy that quadrant only house-banked, via the sweepstakes workaround, per wsc-market-map-toast-only-casino-realtime
